text.title: Drift Classification Taxonomy Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Drift Classification Taxonomy methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Drift class MUST be one of: hand_edit, external_automation, vendor_change, legitimate_emergency, unknown. Custom freeform classes forbidden.
rule.rationale: Closed set keeps routing deterministic; freeform classes calcify into private vocabulary.

rule.statement: Each drift instance MUST carry evidence: cloud audit-log entry, CI run id, person + timestamp, or 'no_evidence_unknown'. Class assignment without evidence is rejected.
rule.rationale: Evidence anchors the classification + prevents 'we'll just call it hand-edit' guessing.

rule.statement: Drift classified legitimate_emergency MUST produce a backport PR to HCL within 5 business days. Open backport > 5d escalates to engineering leader.
rule.rationale: Emergency fixes lost when on-call left = silent regression on next apply.

rule.statement: Drift classified hand_edit defaults to revert (re-apply HCL). Exception (keep the hand-edit) requires backport PR + rationale + named approver.
rule.rationale: Hand-edits are the entry point of drift; revert reinforces the discipline.

rule.statement: Drift classified vendor_change (cloud provider modified defaults) MUST trigger a provider-version bump PR + repro of the new default in HCL. Silent re-apply that hides the change is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Silent override hides vendor behaviour from the team; explicit HCL keeps the codebase honest.

rule.statement: Drift queue MUST be triaged at least weekly. Untriaged items > 14 days escalate to engineering leader + a status report.
rule.rationale: Untriaged drift becomes 'normal'; ritual cadence prevents normalisation.
